creator: n.diablo.n.f@list.ru
text:
Hello! I recently installed FreeBSD 13.1 on an HP ProBook 450 G2 laptop. Installed the latest version of the drm-kmod package. And I ran into a problem: the sound through hdmi sounds slowed down by about half (the video also slows down during playback). This behavior is independent of the sound source (even cat /dev/urandom > /dev/dsp0.0 will be heard slowly). I studied the problem and realized that it is rare, but sometimes it appears. In the Linux environment, it skipped around 2016 and did not pop up after.

Some links:
1. https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/freebsd-13-0-hdmi-audio-slow-motion.79042/page-2
2. https://github.com/FreeBSDDesktop/DEPRECATED-freebsd-base-graphics/issues/36
3. https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/alsa-driver/+bug/1620854
4. https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?id=192628
